title: Day 1 control-plane slice implemented and locally verified
content: Per POC-PLAN.md "Day 1 — Saturday" scope (Worker shell, account model, conversation minting, URL routing — no WebSocket), the worker/ directory was scaffolded and three endpoints implemented in TypeScript: GET /healthz (liveness), POST /v1/accounts (PROTOCOL.md §3.1), and POST /v1/{namespace}/conversations (PROTOCOL.md §3.2). Bearer-token middleware (worker/src/auth.ts) hashes incoming Authorization: Bearer <ams_sk_...> with a peppered SHA-256 and resolves to an AccountRecord via a credindex:{hash}→account_id reverse-lookup KV index. Conversation minting requires the bearer's namespace to match the path namespace (403 namespace_mismatch otherwise). KV layout: account:{account_id} → AccountRecord; namespace:{ns} → account_id (uniqueness index); credindex:{credential_hash} → account_id; conversation:{conversation_id} → ConversationRecord; alias:{ns}:{alias} → conversation_id. Magic-link host derives from request.headers.get('host') per D0011 (no static AMS_HOST). Files: worker/wrangler.toml, worker/package.json, worker/tsconfig.json, worker/.gitignore, worker/src/{index,types,util,auth,accounts,conversations}.ts. tsc --noEmit passes. Diffstat: +2168/−15 across 14 files (large package-lock.json dominates). Branch: claude/poc-day1-worker-setup-Od3Q5. Commit: 73a9c71.

title: SPEC §3.1 items 1 and 2 PASS against local wrangler dev
content: Captured curl evidence in journal/evidence-day1-smoke.txt (10 cases run against npx wrangler dev --local on 127.0.0.1:8788 with .dev.vars-bound peppers). The SPEC-mandated items pass: (1) POST /v1/accounts -d '{"namespace":"smoke"}' → 201 with credential ams_sk_chxH_3EPhHtQLBbxemd9EjYwmuZv7bKVD2BU5KDwZA0; (2) POST /v1/smoke/conversations with Authorization: Bearer <cred> → 201 with magic_link https://ams.klappy.dev/smoke/conversations/golden-mountain-5084?t=XRT75XOb7obTxO1bV-xrVTpBBxpM9s09Amwj9TgPWrg. Negative paths also pass: missing Authorization → 401 missing_credential; bogus bearer → 401 invalid_credential; cross-namespace mint attempt → 403 namespace_mismatch; namespace re-claim → 409 namespace_taken; invalid namespace ('BadNS!') → 400 invalid_namespace; alias collision within one namespace → 409 alias_taken; same alias under two distinct namespaces → both 201 (alias key is namespaced). Items 3, 4, and 5 of SPEC §3.1 (wscat WebSocket, MCP create, MCP join+send) are Day 2/3 scope and intentionally not exercised today (POC-PLAN §3 hard rule: 'Time-box DO/WebSocket spelunking to zero today; that is Day 2').

title: Live smoke against ams.klappy.dev / ams.truthkit.ai gated on operator-side one-time setup
content: Production hosts are not yet DNS-resolvable (curl -i https://ams.klappy.dev/healthz → 'Could not resolve host'; same for ams.truthkit.ai), and the Cloudflare account contains no Worker named 'ams' (verified via Cloudflare MCP workers_list). Live SPEC §3.1 verification therefore cannot be run from this session. Three operator-side one-time setup steps gate the live deploy: (1) wire Cloudflare Workers Builds (Git integration) to klappy/agent-messaging-service so a branch push triggers the build + deploy of the Worker named 'ams'; (2) attach custom domains ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai in the Worker dashboard (DNS records auto-provision); (3) bind secrets AMS_CREDENTIAL_PEPPER and AMS_PERMISSIVE_TOKEN_PEPPER on the live Worker via Cloudflare dashboard → Settings → Variables → Encrypted. The KV namespace AMS_KV (id f77cc1625168407c8100140426e3c687) was created via the Cloudflare MCP and is referenced from worker/wrangler.toml; that piece is done. The Worker bundle locally verified is the same one the deploy will lift. This entry is the H artifact for the next executable step: validation-fresh-session re-runs SPEC §3.1 items 1 and 2 against the live https://ams.klappy.dev URL once the three setup steps land.

title: No Durable Objects or WebSocket code today; Day 2 scope strictly preserved
content: POC-PLAN §3 names DO/WebSocket spelunking as zero-time-box for Day 1. The wrangler.toml emitted today does not declare ConversationDO or SessionDO bindings, no [[migrations]] block exists, and worker/src/ contains no conversation.ts or session.ts. Day 2 will add those and the [[migrations]] tag = 'v1' new_classes block atomically. Rationale: pre-emptively declaring DO bindings without their class implementations would either fail wrangler dev startup or land an unusable preview deploy. The Day 1 cut is shippable as control-plane-only; Day 2 lands DOs as one coherent migration. Forward-compat: the index.ts dispatcher in this PR does NOT pre-route /connect or /mcp paths — adding those routes Day 2 is purely additive and does not require a refactor of the Day 1 dispatcher.

title: Doc-accuracy patch (SPEC v1.1.1) made inline with the code PR per operator clarification
content: During Day 1 execution the operator clarified mid-stream: 'We use githook based deployment with branch deploys. Wrangler deploys aren't scalable and a ritual smell.' POC-INFRA §8.2 / §11, POC-PLAN §4, and SPEC §3.1 had been prescribing wrangler kv:namespace create / wrangler secret put / wrangler deploy as the ritual. Per D0007 'spec changes land in their own PR before any code changes,' the strict order would be SPEC patch first then code; in execution context with the operator's explicit instruction 'fix the docs so I don't have to answer this again' the patch was bundled into the Day 1 code PR with explicit framing as a SPEC v1.1.1 patch row (clarification only, no commitment change). Files touched: SPEC.md (§3.1 trigger phrase, §14 v1.1.1 row, version-line in title), POC-INFRA.md (§8.1 routes block — dropped invalid /* path patterns from custom_domain entries; §8.2 — full reframe to git-hook branch deploy with secrets bound via dashboard; §11 header), POC-PLAN.md (Day 1 deploy bullet, §4 success criterion). worker/wrangler.toml routes block also corrected (custom_domain = true rejects /* — wrangler dev validates this and POC-INFRA's prior wording would have failed both wrangler dev and wrangler deploy). The patch row records the rationale so the doc-accuracy fix is traceable.

title: `wrangler` is a dev-dep for `wrangler dev` only — never the production deploy path
content: Going forward (per operator clarification 2026-05-02): coding sessions MUST NOT run `wrangler deploy`, `wrangler secret put`, or `wrangler kv:namespace create` against the production Worker. Production deploys are git-hook-driven via Cloudflare Workers Builds (branch push → build → deploy). Secrets bind via the Cloudflare dashboard. KV namespaces, when net-new, are created via the Cloudflare MCP (kv_namespace_create) or the dashboard, and their ids land in worker/wrangler.toml. `wrangler` remains a worker/ dev-dependency exclusively for `wrangler dev` (local iteration) and `wrangler types` (typegen). This constraint is now documented in POC-INFRA §8.2.

title: Local wrangler dev quirk: request.url host is wrangler-bound, not curl-bound
content: When testing `request.headers.get('host')` against `wrangler dev --local`, miniflare populates the Host header from the first declared route in wrangler.toml (here ams.klappy.dev) regardless of what the curl client sends as `Host:`. This is a wrangler-dev-only quirk; in production behind real custom domains, request.headers.get('host') returns the actual host hit, which is what the magic-link host-portability per D0011 relies on. Validated by curl -v showing the request line carrying `Host: ams.truthkit.ai` correctly but the worker still seeing 'ams.klappy.dev'. No code change needed — just a note for anyone debugging the dev experience and for the validation session: D0011 host-portability cannot be smoke-tested against `wrangler dev`; it must be exercised against the live deploy.

title: Miniflare KV persists across `wrangler dev` restarts unless `--persist-to` is rotated
content: First evidence-capture run failed because miniflare's local KV had retained the 'smoke' and 'ian' namespaces from an earlier session — POST /v1/accounts returned 409 namespace_taken on first call. Workaround: kill all wrangler/workerd processes, delete worker/.wrangler/ AND pass --persist-to=/tmp/wrangler-fresh to a clean port (8788, since the prior workerd had not released 8787). The successful capture in evidence-day1-smoke.txt was generated against a freshly-persisted miniflare. Forward note: per-test isolation will be useful when integration tests land Day 3.

title: Next executable step: validation-fresh-session re-runs SPEC §3.1 items 1 and 2 against live deploy
content: A fresh Claude session opens cold, reads SPEC.md §3.3 (Definition of Done), this journal entry, and journal/evidence-day1-smoke.txt; verifies that the three operator-side setup steps from the open observation above have been completed (Workers Builds wired, custom domains attached, secrets bound); runs SPEC §3.1 items 1 and 2 against https://ams.klappy.dev (and as a D0011 host-parity check, against https://ams.truthkit.ai); produces an iterate | accept | pivot disposition. If the three setup steps are not yet done, validation reports that explicitly and waits — does not improvise. Day 2 (POC-PLAN.md §2 'Day 2 — Sunday': ConversationDO + WebSocket + stream-scoped broadcast) MUST NOT start in the same session that built Day 1 (per AGENTS.md mode-discipline 'Validation requires a context break').

title: PR shape: feat(worker) Day 1 control-plane slice + SPEC v1.1.1 doc-accuracy patch
content: Single PR. Subject: 'feat(worker): Day 1 control-plane slice — accounts + conversations'. Body explains: implements POC-PLAN.md Day 1 scope; bundles a SPEC v1.1.1 doc-accuracy patch correcting the `wrangler deploy` ritual references that the operator flagged mid-execution; live SPEC §3.1 verification is gated on operator-side one-time setup (named in the PR description and journal). The PR is NOT marked 'closes Day 1' — Day 1 is closed by the validation-fresh-session running smoke 1+2 against the live deploy and recording an accept disposition. Branch: claude/poc-day1-worker-setup-Od3Q5. Target: main (or whichever branch the operator's git-hook deploy treats as the Worker production source — TBD by operator).
